entry.title: Just Flight Releases Cessna 152 for X-Plane 12
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. Just Flight has recently released the Cessna 152 for X-Plane 12, developed in partnership with Thranda Design. After bringing its PA-28 fleet over, Just Flight has now given the same treatment to the popular two-seat trainer, with a new XP12-native version. The aircraft is based on the real-world airframe G-BGAE, based at Conington Airfield, and comes with six paint schemes covering operators from the UK, the United States, Switzerland, Italy, Germany, and Czechia. Thranda’s Dynamic Livery system is included too, letting users create their own paint schemes from within the EFB. The C152 has PBR materials, real-time reflections, 8K textures, HDR lighting, native X-Plane 12 rain effects, and a long list of animations, including doors, windows, vents, sun visors, and even an ignition keychain that moves with G-forces. Inside the cockpit, Just Flight has modelled a full avionics suite, VR support, custom engine and electrical logic, interactive checklists and a feature-rich EFB with weight and balance tools, aircraft options, engine condition pages, and the Thranda livery controls. Just Flight also highlights a custom-coded electrical system with working circuit breakers, vapour lock and spark plug fouling simulation, custom sounds, and updated flight dynamics built around X-Plane 12’s newer flight model. ‍ MODEL COCKPIT AIRCRAFT SYSTEMS LIVERIESThe C152 is supplied in the following six paint schemes: OTHER FEATURES If you already own the X-Plane 11 version, Just Flight says a 25% discount is available. The aircraft costs $42.99 USD for new customers, and you can purchase it here. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: iniBuilds Updates TriStar Airliner for MSFS 2024 to v1.0.1
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. iniBuilds has released the first update for their newly released L1011 TriStar Airliner, version 1.0.1. It is a mostly quality-of-life update, refining many aspects of the new release and improving stability and user experience. On the systems side, iniBuilds has worked through a long list of fixes across the INS, annunciators, gear lever behaviour, VNAV and PMS performance, ILS and flight director logic, cabin temperature, pressurisation, anti-ice indications, spoiler behaviour, ground spoilers, autobrake simulation, auto throttle disconnect logic, and more. Now included are animated circuit breakers, tied into the electrical system, plus tooltips for them. As for artwork, iniBuilds has fixed the floating wipers issue, dark cockpit window tint, displaced weathering, wing z-fighting, incorrect jetbridge behaviour on non-lounge variants, and a hole near the APU exhaust. Some liveries have also been corrected, including placards, logos, and cabin decals, and, in one of the more random additions in the changelog, lavatory doors are now operable. Brightness on the take-off card is now tied to EFB priority selection, auto-complete on start now matches the calculated flap setting more closely, and overall night brightness has been reduced. iniBuilds has also added the ability to switch between take-off and landing cards by pressing the top of the card. As for soundscape, there is a fix targeting incorrect V1 and rotate call behaviour. ‍ V1.0.1SYSTEMS ⚙️FIXED INS MSU not detented to/from NAV positionFIXED Light test switch doesn't fully illuminate all annunciators (especially on Main Instrument Panel)FIXED Cabin climb indicator makes occasional leaps up & down when sitting still on the groundFIXED Gear lever desyncs when using peripheralsFIXED TM not respecting PMS constraints at calculated valueFIXED Speed vertical modes need to command pitch much more readilyFIXED ILS mode remains on with AP/FD offFIXED FD ILS LOC guidance unsatisfactoryFIXED Very cold cabin temperatureFIXED PMS VNAV control is not fast or accurate enough leading to consistent speed under/overshootsFIXED Cabin lights remain on with no elec. powerFIXED Engine 1 & 3 anti ice ON not illuminating HEATFIXED Pressurization PSI needle animation issueFIXED TOGA flag still missing from AFCS mode annunciatorFIXED Incorrect ATS DISC flag when using TMFIXED Spoiler lever retracts automatically after landingFIXED Ground spoilers not extending fully on reverse selectionFIXED Cabin heat not increasing past indicated limitFIXED Autobrake simulationFIXED Fire detection loop test knobs & Fire bell sound.FIXED Nacelle/pylon ovht detection test knobs & emissive.FIXED Engine turbine cooling air panel test emissive.FIXED Wheel well fire test panelFIXED Humidity control panel button animation.FIXED Forward cargo compartment logic for guarded switches with labels.FIXED N2 test buttons & logicFIXED Engine oil, temp & press test buttons & logicFIXED #3 Engine TGT & all Celsius Gauges INOPFIXED Rudder Axis binding issue for gamepad controllerFIXED FMA test doesn't work on F/O sideFIXED Fire Bell cutout requires subsequent press to silence alarmFIXED Walkaround - ACU disconnects immediately after clicking on air ventFIXED Auto throttle not disconnecting when weight on wheels has above nil valueFIXED SPOILER ON/OFF control commands not workingADDED Circuit breaker animation and wired to basic electrical system functions.ADDED Tooltips to all CB. ART 🎨FIXED Wipers are floating above windshieldFIXED Standby altimeter animation errorFIXED ADF freq has no integral lightFIXED Transponder digits are brighter than comm radiosFIXED Excessive weathering effectFIXED Jetbridge attempts to connect to lounge even in non-lounge variantsFIXED Door profile incomplete? See referenceFIXED Wipers do not wipe away moisture on windshieldFIXED Covert sunshades to draggable elementFIXED Mechanic collision with ENG1 doorsFIXED Wipers in any LOD other than 0 are in the old parked positionFIXED Hole in rear of fuselage near APU exhaustFIXED Cockpit window tint too darkFIXED Cargo doors weathering displaced, floating above modelFIXED Aircraft Weathering not parented to door animationsFIXED Weathering elements on tail floating above fuselageFIXED z-fighting on wings with weathering enabledFIXED Cathay VR-HHX - Door decals in cabinFIXED Novair - Logo mirrored and font accuracyFIXED All Liveries - Cockpit Placard Shows G-STAR / AB-CD Instead of Actual Reg/SelcalADDED Operable lavatory doors PFB📜FIXED Tie Takeoff card brightness to selected EFB as decided by priority switchFIXED Auto-Complete on start doesn't match flaps from calculated valueFIXED Reduced overall brightness at nightADDED Can now switch between T/O & Land cards with top of card press SOUNDS🔊FIXED v1 & rotate calls when play when decelerating to bugged speed The update is available now through the MSFS 2024 in-sim Marketplace. It can be purchased for $49.99 USD. The aircraft is not yet out on the iniBuilds Store or other vendors. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Parallel 42 Releases ChasePlane SU5 Beta Build for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Parallel 42 has released the first experimental ChasePlane build, now with Sim Update 5 beta support for MSFS 2024. The news was shared in a blog post on Parallel 42's website. According to the team, the last six months have been spent on refining the camera add-on, fixing various issues, and working around sim limitations. Among the biggest changes is the incorporation of Asobo’s new Camera API. Parallel 42 says it began reworking ChasePlane shortly after Asobo announced the new camera tools for Sim Update 5, and this beta build already uses the new system where the older code was no longer enough. The move also puts ChasePlane onto the framework it will need going forward, with the new Camera API giving Parallel 42 a much clearer path towards VR support in MSFS 2024, something that has been harder to achieve than in the MSFS 2020 version. Parallel 42 says work on VR for MSFS 2024 will begin once Sim Update 5 leaves beta. Sim Update 5 support comes with the downside of the copilot avatar no longer fading away when the camera gets too close, which means some view presets may clip into the model. You can fix this by nudging the view forward or removing the copilot entirely in the settings. The issue has already been reported to Asobo, and the team expects it to be addressed in a later Sim Update 5 beta build or early in the Sim Update 6 cycle. With SU5, toolbar add-ons, such as ChasePlane, now work in Career Mode as well, which removes one of the biggest limitations the add-on has had in MSFS 2024 since launch. If you want to try the new experimental build, download the latest ChasePlane Manager from your store account. The addon is available for $30.00 USD on the P42 store. The known limitations page has also been updated with the new release. Keep in mind that this is an experimental release, and that things may break again with future beta releases from Microsoft. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: PMDG Shares 737-700 Release Trailer for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. PMDG has released a new preview trailer for the 737-700NG for MSFS 2024, just a day before the aircraft is set to launch on April 9. Created by AviatorCro, the video showcases more of PMDG’s “High Detail Update”, an effort to bring more detailed artwork and texturing to the 737 lineup for MSFS 2024. PMDG first announced this overhaul back in November 2025, and, after what they say was a longer wait than planned, the work is now complete for the -700. According to PMDG, the update brings an improved level of detail across the aircraft, better textures and geometry, reworked skin mapping, more exterior animations, updated lighting, and extra detail throughout the cabin, cockpit, cargo holds, landing gear, gear bays, engines, and ground equipment. The 737-700 will be the first variant to release with the completed High Detail Update on April 9, with the 737-600, 737-800, and 737-900 due to receive the same treatment through regular product updates later this month. As with the other 737 releases, owners of the MSFS 2020 737-700 will be eligible for a discount when buying the MSFS 2024 version, whether through PMDG or the in-sim Marketplace. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: FlightSim Studio AG Releases E-Jets Experimental Update
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. FlightSim Studio AG has released a new public experimental update for its E-Jets series of aircraft, with the developer and beta team pushing the aircraft a bit further over the past few weeks. The main additions in this update are holdings and temperature compensation for RNAV approaches, both now supported through the MCDU. Beyond that, the changelog points to wider work across the FMS and lateral navigation, with big MCDU and LNAV improvements, a full missed approach implementation, and support for completing multiple legs through the flight complete phase. FlightSim Studio AG also says take-off and landing behaviour has been improved, alongside what it describes as “tons of smaller fixes and improvements”. They also used the update to point out what is still seen as the last big milestone, the autopilot. FlightSim Studio AG says it works in general, but still not as accurately as the team wants, and does not yet meet their own expectations. Right now, the system is still built on top of the default Microsoft Flight Simulator autopilot, with a custom code layer added over it. Long-term, the plan is to replace that with a fully custom autopilot. ‍ **Changelog Version 0.10.36** - Major MCDU & LNAV improvements - Improved takeoff & landing behaviour - Full missed approach implementation - FMS temperature compensation for RNAV approaches - MCDU Holdings with all entry types - MCDU flight complete phase allows multiple legs in one session - Tons of smaller fixes and improvements ‍ Full post and Release Notes The new build, version 0.10.36, is available through the experimental branches on Contrail and Aerosoft One. The E190/E195 and E170/E175 aircraft are available for $39.23 USD through the same retailers. There is also a freighter version. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Echo 19 Teases Cessna 208B EX Precision Audio Pack for MSFS2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Echo 19 has previewed its Cessna 208B EX Precision Audio Pack for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, delivering an immersive and highly responsive audio experience for the default Asobo C208B EX Grand Caravan. Like with the previous Echo 19’s products, the Grand Caravan audio pack is also built using the EchoFX, an in-house system that enables the audio to respond to G-forces, weather, altitude, flight physics and state of the aircraft. The soundpack has been recorded from the real aircraft with over 12 different microphones, and features custom PT6 engine sounds, 360° propeller audio, interior sounds, system sounds (such as the AC vent fans, bleed-air heating, and the flap motor), and a headphone simulation function. Aside from the sounds, the pack also includes 9 different liveries. The full feature list can be read below: 🔥 All-New PT6 Engine Audio 🌀 Propeller Audio in Full Surround 🛩️ Deeply Modeled 208B EX Characteristics 🌡️ System Sounds (New From Default) 🎙️ Full-3D Cockpit Interactivity 🎚️ Smart Customization Controls 🌎 Rich Exterior Ambience 🎨 Bonus Content ✝️ Flying for a Cause ‍ While the release date still isn’t known, the C208B EX Precision Audio Pack is priced at $13.25 on the Echo 19 store. ‍ For all the latest news and updates, stay tuned on Threshold! ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: BeyondATC Releases Stage One of VFR on Experimental Branch
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Drzewiecki Design has released EPSC Szczecin for Microsoft Flight Simulator, with the scenery now available for both MSFS 2020 and MSFS 2024. The new package models Solidarity Szczecin-Goleniów Airport, the main airport serving Szczecin, north-western Poland, and part of the wider Baltic region. The airport sits approximately 28 miles from Szczecin, and, while it is on the smaller side, it plays an important regional role in Poland, handling scheduled, seasonal, and charter traffic. Drzewiecki Design also points out the airport’s military roots, with the field gradually shifting into civilian use over time, before becoming the regional gateway it is today. The package includes accurate aprons, taxiways, stands, markings, lighting, landside areas, technical infrastructure, and a broad set of custom assets built specifically for the airport. It features high-quality modelling and texturing, custom runway, taxiway, and approach lighting, immersive clutter and interiors, and a performance-friendly build, with optimised geometry, LODs, and textures The developer also says the scenery has been designed to work properly with AI traffic and ATC addons. ‍ SCENERY FEATURES ‍ An interesting sidenote is that the scenery includes the airport’s planned passenger terminal, which is expected to open in 2027. According to Drzewiecki Design, that terminal has been recreated from architectural plans and official visualisations, while the airport layout itself still reflects the current real-world configuration. Drzewiecki Design’s Solidarity Szczecin–Goleniów Airport is available through a range of retailers, including iniBuilds, Contrail, Aerosoft, Orbx, simMarket and Just Flight, for roughly $17 USD. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Drzewiecki Design Releases Solidarity Szczecin–Goleniów Airport for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Drzewiecki Design has released EPSC Szczecin for Microsoft Flight Simulator, with the scenery now available for both MSFS 2020 and MSFS 2024. The new package models Solidarity Szczecin-Goleniów Airport, the main airport serving Szczecin, north-western Poland, and part of the wider Baltic region. The airport sits approximately 28 miles from Szczecin, and, while it is on the smaller side, it plays an important regional role in Poland, handling scheduled, seasonal, and charter traffic. Drzewiecki Design also points out the airport’s military roots, with the field gradually shifting into civilian use over time, before becoming the regional gateway it is today. The package includes accurate aprons, taxiways, stands, markings, lighting, landside areas, technical infrastructure, and a broad set of custom assets built specifically for the airport. It features high-quality modelling and texturing, custom runway, taxiway, and approach lighting, immersive clutter and interiors, and a performance-friendly build, with optimised geometry, LODs, and textures The developer also says the scenery has been designed to work properly with AI traffic and ATC addons. ‍ SCENERY FEATURES An interesting sidenote is that the scenery includes the airport’s planned passenger terminal, which is expected to open in 2027. According to Drzewiecki, that terminal has been recreated from architectural plans and official visualisations, while the airport layout itself still reflects the current real-world configuration. Drzewiecki Design’s Solidarity Szczecin–Goleniów Airport is available through a range of retailers, including iniBuilds, Contrail, Aerosoft, Orbx, simMarket and Just Flight, for roughly $17 USD. It is compatible with existing Asobo scenery. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Verticalsim Announces John Wayne Airport for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Verticalsim has announced its next airport project, and this time in Southern California, with KSNA, John Wayne Airport, for MSFS 2024. The developer says this is one of three airports currently in development. Set close to Disneyland and in the middle of a busy region in Southern California, John Wayne is known for a short runway and stringent noise abatement procedures. The traffic there includes a mix of airliners, business jets and general aviation. Verticalsim says to expect high-detail terminal modelling, Clay Lacy Aviation’s FBO, and an accurate ground layout with correct markings. The developer says the project is being built around a “true-to-life” Southern California atmosphere, which may entail a lot of things. They did not give much preview imagery, only linking a few real-life photos, in addition to a very early render. Safe to say, it is early stages for the project. Verticalsim also says the project is benefiting from collaboration with local ramp agents and airport personnel, with the aim of giving the scenery stronger real-world accuracy and operational detail. There is no release window yet, but more information, along with in-sim previews, is expected soon. ‍ ‍If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: FlightFactor Shares More 777-300ER Images, Paint Kit Now Available
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. FlightFactor has shared more preview images of its upcoming Boeing 777-300ER for X-Plane 12 through its Discord, and while there is still not much in the way of new hard information, we do get a range of different images. They also say that the paint kit is now out, which usually means the aircraft is getting closer, and that repainters can start getting to work before release. FlightFactor still has not shared a release date, but it does look like the 300ER is very close to release. ‍ ‍If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: MK Studios Releases Porto Airport for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. After teasing it over the past week, MK Studios has today released LPPR Porto Airport for MSFS 2024. In its release post, MK Studios describes the airport as a detailed recreation of the real-world airfield. The scenery models Francisco Sa Carneiro Airport, the main international gateway to northern Portugal, just northwest of Porto city itself. The developer says the airport includes an up-to-date layout based on current real-world data, detailed terminal interiors and exteriors, custom high-resolution ground textures with PBR materials, and animated jetways. It also comes with accurate stand layouts, signage, and ground markings, alongside realistic night lighting, stop bars, and approach lighting. MK Studios also says the scenery has been optimised carefully, so high visual quality has been achieved without hitting performance harder than it needs to. ‍ ‍ Full product page The release is exclusive to MSFS 2024, with owners of the older MSFS 2020 version able to upgrade at a discounted price. The airport is available through Contrail, simMarket, Orbx, and iniBuilds for roughly $20 USD. ‍ ‍If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Burning Blue Design Releases Cotswold Airport, Kemble for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Burning Blue Design has today released Cotswold Airport, Kemble, for MSFS 2020 and 2024. Set in Gloucestershire, just south-west of Cirencester, Kemble started life as RAF Kemble and dates back to 1936. During the Second World War, it played an important role in maintenance, preparation, and ferry work, and later became known as the long-time home of the Red Arrows from 1966 to 1983. In more recent years, the site has evolved into Cotswold Airport, a private aviation hub with training activity, business aviation, engineering facilities, and rows of stored and retired airliners. Burning Blue Design says it carried out on-site visits to make sure the scenery feels accurate. The airport includes over 200 custom 3D objects with PBR textures, enhanced ground textures, custom taxi signs, weather compatibility, and full native support for both versions of Microsoft Flight Simulator. There are detailed hangar and control room interiors, animated hangar doors, custom windsocks, animated spectators, and parking spots ranging from microlights to the large airliners. Again, a cool part of this scenery is the rows of parked airliners. Alongside stored Airbus A319s and Boeing 737s, to represent Kemble’s recycling and storage role, Burning Blue Design has included several locally relevant aircraft, such as the Negus-liveried Boeing 747 G-CIVB, a Red Arrows Folland Gnat, a Buccaneer, Phantom, Mustang, and several light aircraft based around the field Full product page ‍ Cotswold Airport, Kemble, is available now from Burning Blue Design’s website for $21,44 USD, with releases on Orbx and the in-sim Marketplace set to follow. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: PMDG Releases 737-700 for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. PMDG has today released the 737-700 for MSFS 2024, after teasing it earlier this month. The release lands on a symbolic date for the developer, arriving 23 years after the original 737NG for FS2002 entered beta, back in 2003. In their release post, PMDG says the result is its most in-depth desktop 737 simulation yet, and one that now fully embraces MSFS 2024 as its platform going forward. While PMDG has already been bringing its 737NG line over from MSFS 2020 for a while now, the -700 is the first variant to ship with the so-called “High Detail Update”. First announced in Nov. 2025, this upgrade steps up the visual quality of the aircraft using 2024 features, with improved geometry, better textures, updated lighting, a more detailed cabin and cargo hold, reworked gear bays, better GSE, and a jump in smaller surface detail throughout the airframe. The -700 has acted as the testbed for this broader visual overhaul, and the same changes will roll out across the already released -600, -800, and -900 via regular product updates in the days ahead. The aircraft comes with the usual passenger, BBJ, and BDSF freighter variants, Blended and Split Scimitar winglet options, and with an enhanced cabin, updated soundcape, EFB tablet with Navigraph compatibility and datalink options including SimBrief integration. PMDG highlights lidar-scanned models, upgraded materials, rigid body physics for wing and tail surfaces, and further use of MSFS 2024-specific techniques to improve both fidelity and longevity. As with the move from older platforms to MSFS 2020, PMDG is again offering upgrade pricing for existing customers. Anyone who already owns the PMDG 737-700 for MSFS 2020 through PMDG.com will receive 60% off the new version at checkout, as long as they are signed into the correct account before purchasing. Marketplace customers will receive the same discount once the aircraft arrives there as well. The PMDG 737-700 for MSFS 2024 is available from PMDG.com for $74.99 USD. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: SimPixel Releases Den Helder Airport for MSFS 2020
entry.summary: Sim: MSFS20. SimPixel has just launched its version of Den Helder Airport (EHKD) for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020. This civil and naval airport is situated in the town of Den Helder, in the northern Netherlands, and has a runway that is 4,183 feet long. The airport, built in 1918, was originally established as a base for the Royal Netherlands Navy. During the Second World War, it was under German control but returned to its original purpose as a base for the Royal Netherlands Navy after the war, albeit with some necessary reparations. In 1960, the airport received a concrete runway, having previously operated on a grass field. In the 1980s, as the oil rigs in the North Sea began to require helicopter services, the military started to share the airport with civilian users. Since then, it has served both military and civilian purposes. The scenery from SimPixel includes high-resolution satellite imagery, custom 3D models, and dynamic lighting, among other features. The newly released Den Helder Airport for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 is now available from iniBuilds for approximately $9.40. ‍ For the latest flight simulation news, remember to stay tuned on Threshold! ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: X-Crafts Updates ERJ Family for X-Plane 12
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. X-Crafts has released an update for the ERJ family. The v2.1.0 is the first update since the release in February, and brings a long list of improvements and bug fixes. The update has introduced many avionics features that were previously missing (such as VOR FMA, HSI drift indicator, PLI stall warning), sound improvements (the sounds now have smoother dynamic curves, and the cabin announcements, the TCAS test and the Landing Gear warning sounds have been added), AirComms tutorials, improvements to the spoilers, and various bug fixes. The full changelog is as follows: AVIONICS Improved: VECTOR waypoint now displays accurately at 20 nautical miles instead of hundreds of miles away. Improved: Enhanced ITT throttle response to prevent erratic changes. ITT is much more stable now. Improved: Vspeed setting now allows values below initial minimums after first set. Added: VOR mode annunciator on the FMA Added: Drift indicator to the HSI Added: PLI (Bart Simpson hair) has been added as a visual stall warning on the PFD Fixed: GS vertical deviation pointer now visible for NAV2 frequencies on both PFDs Fixed: HSI needles on PFD2 were tied to PFD1 brightness Fixed: Magenta Glidepath deviation now displays correctly on PFD2 when FMS1 is the source. Fixed: Box around DATA on MFD2 now correctly disappears when waypoints are hidden. Fixed: Navmap freezing on wrong data wpts. Fixed: TKNB indication corrected to display on both PFDs when CPL is set to CA side with AP engaged. Fixed: AHRS alignment on PFD2 is no longer tied to the brightness knob for PFD1. GPWS Improved: New GPWS system with 2 configurations: Default: 500 - 200 - Approaching minimums - Minimums - 100 Advanced: 500 - Approaching minimums - minimums - 100 - 50 - 40 - 30 - 20 - 10 Note that the “500” is a smart callout that would not be called on an ILS approach within normal parameters. It would be triggered if LOC/GP deviation is > 2 dots, or if no valid ILS is tuned. The 200 callout is muted if minimums is also set to 200. Added: GPWS TERRAIN SYS OVRD button to mute ground proximity warnings. NAV MAP Improved: Resource usage reduced in replay mode for xnavmap. Fixed: xnavmap now accepts negative heading values, preventing crashes. Fixed: An issue where the route and symbols on the map disappeared. AUTOPILOT Fixed: Bank angle switchover at 14 degrees above 25000 feet and 27 degrees below 24750 feet. SPOILERS / BRAKES Improved: Spoilers no longer move without hydraulic power and now function according to AOM specifications. Improved: The speedbrake lever now only has an open or closed position, as on the real airplane. Added: See our hardware setup guidelines. Fixed: Ground spoilers now deploy correctly regardless of lever handle position. Fixed: Speedbrakes now automatically close if the throttle is not at idle. Fixed: Parking brake now engages properly even when the toe brakes are applied. SYSTEMS Improved: Fuel system updated to more efficiently heat the fuel, preventing LOW FUEL TEMP message from coming up when it shouldn't Fixed: Bleed low temp message now displays correctly during engines off test A. Fixed: Cabin Altitude indication changes color for high cabin altitude Fixed: Fuel oil heat exchanger properly configured to prevent FUEL LOW EICAS message. Fixed: No ICE/AI ON caution message is now accurately displayed only when wing and stab valves open. COCKPIT / INTERACTION Improved: Click spots for the pop up TRS panel repositioned Fixed: Reversed the altitude selector click spot on the pressurization panel Fixed: Throttles in the cockpit move backward correctly when in reverse. Fixed: Cycle TRS command now correctly cycles between CRZ and CLB during cruise without engaging CON. Fixed: The 1/2 bank button now properly illuminates only in HDG mode. Fixed: Tiller animation is now smooth EFB Added: Configurable GPWS callouts to the EFB Sound page, providing Default and Expanded GPWS options. Added: Expanded GPWS settings to the Settings Tab Fixed: EFB 2D window no longer opens empty and gray when Avitab is active on the 3D. AIRCOMMS Added: All tutorials are now available Fixed: Aircomms not loading correctly for some users, resulting in no sounds and EFB failures. Fixed: Getting Started message now loads on first two loads SOUNDS Major improvements to the sound experience with new sounds, updated dynamic volume curves, and smoother transitions, including revised engine startup, engine exterior sounds, and avionics fan sounds. Enabled Cabin Announcements Placed a more restrictive limiter on reverse thrust and interior wind sounds. Revised Sound spaces to eliminate GPU volume glitch and smooth transition from cockpit to cabin. Revised exterior sounds and volume curves to make a more dynamic spool up. Replaced exterior engine startup sounds. Revised primary avionic fans to lessen the low-tone pulsing sound. Replaced altitude callouts. Replaced the minimum callout. Adjusted/fixed exterior engine audible distances. Added the Doppler effect on exterior engines. Added TCAS test (place on TEST for 5 seconds). Added TCAS traffic. Changed thrust reverse dataref for more reliable deployment of sound. Adjusted exterior blast to wrap further around so as not to be such a narrow area behind the plane. Further interior balance between cockpit/cabin sound spaces for smooth transition with running engines. Changed the electric hydraulic logic to play when both engines are shut down. Added dedicated SPS event. Added Landing Gear not down warning: Condition 1: Radio altitude is below 1,200 ft + Flap selector lever set to 0° or 9° + Low thrust Condition 2: Radio altitude is below 1,200 ft + Flap selector lever set to 22° or 45° + Low thrust. This warning cannot be inhibited. Condition 3: Any time the flap selector lever is set to 45° (even at a high altitude or with high thrust). This warning cannot be inhibited. 3D MODEL / EXTERIOR Improved: Repositioned docking position for jetways to prevent cockpit intersection Improved: Enhanced Wingflex behavior to prevent excessive flexing in negative lift force conditions across all wings. Fixed: Removed the service door click spot on the Legacy 650 Fixed: American Eagle logo repositioned for full visibility on the E135 fuselage. CABIN Fixed: Seatbelt signs are now tied to the correct switch LIGHTING Fixed: Cabin lights and cockpit dome lights now work with ground power. TCAS Added: Enabled the TST button on the RMU for the TCAS system TEST Fixed: TCAS functionality will stay active instead of going to standby in version 12.4.1 Beta. CHECKLISTS Improved: Updated checklists to better reflect real-world operations CTDs Fixed: All known crash-to-desktop (CTD) issues that were replicated have been resolved. The v2.1.0 update can be downloaded from the X-Plane.org store or from the Skunkcrafts updater. The X-Crafts’ ERJ Family for X-Plane 12 is available for purchase on the X-Plane.org store as a bundle for $139.95 or individually: $69.95 for E145/E145XR/Legacy 650 and $59.95 for E135 and E140. ‍ For the latest flight simulation news, remember to stay tuned on Threshold! If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: Propair Flight Releases Brussels-Charleroi Airport for MSFS2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Propair Flight have released their rendition of the Brussels South Charleroi Airport (EBCI/CRL) for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024. Located 7 kilometres from the city of Charleroi, and 46km from Brussels, it serves both Charleroi and Brussels. The history of Charleroi in aviation began back in 1919, when the first flight school was opened on the highest point of the region. Shortly after, the airfield also received a maintenance facility. In 1931, British aircraft producer, Fairfey Aviation, opened a subsidiary in Gosselies, making it one of the important centres of aviation in Europe. During WWII, for a short period of time, it served as an airfield for Allied air forces. After the war, the airport was owned and operated by a government agency, but just like pre-war, it was still a home for aircraft manufacturing. In the 1970s, Sabena, the then-Belgian flag carrier, launched a route from Liege to Gatwick via Charleroi, but it was soon suspended due to poor results. Until the late 1990s, the Charleroi airport saw little to no passenger traffic and was mostly used for general aviation. That was, until 1997, when the airline market in the EU was deregulated, and when Ryanair opened their first base in continental Europe in Charleroi. Currently, the Brussels South Charleroi Airport is operated by three low-cost airlines: Ryanair, Wizz Air and Pegasus Airlines, with flights to over 100 destinations across Europe and northwestern Africa, with additional seasonal service by Air Arabia, Air Corsica and Volotea. The Propair Flight’s rendition of Charleroi features high-resolution PBR textures, custom ground polygons, animated people, custom dynamic night lighting, highly-detailed custom buildings, accurate airport layout, and modelled terminal interior. The scenery is available for purchase on the Propair Flight store and Orbx marketplace for $17.98. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍
